articles.title: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri likely to visit Nepal this month
articles.description: As per sources, dates for the visit are being finalised. The Ministry of External Affairs is yet to make an official announcement in this regard.

articles.title: Hegseth ‘HUMILIATED’ Over ‘Christianity Push’ In US Military; Senator Reed 'EXPOSES' Trump Aide
articles.description: A pointed exchange unfolded on Capitol Hill as Senator Jack Reed questioned Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth during a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing over religious inclusion within the U.S. military. Reed pressed on whether the Department of Defense is ensuring equal acceptance of all faiths among service members, raising concerns about religious balance, policy, and leadership messaging. The moment highlights a broader debate over diversity, religious freedom, and the role of faith in the armed forces, as scrutiny grows over how inclusive military culture and policies truly are.

articles.title: Iran 'will not capitulate', conflict resolution expert says
articles.description: Iran's supreme leader declared Thursday that the United States had suffered a shameful defeat, defiantly rejecting a warning from President Donald Trump that an economically punishing US naval blockade could be enforced for months to come. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Oliver McTernan, director and co-founder of Forward Thinking, says that Iran "will not capitulate" adding that "it was a miscalculation rushing in an illicit aggression against Iran".

articles.title: Rajnath Singh meets Italy’s Guido Crosetto, sets the course for defence cooperation
articles.description: During the meeting, Singh is also learnt to have conveyed India’s concerns over transfer of sensitive defence technologies to Pakistan.

articles.title: 56-year-old man charred to death in LPG cylinder explosion near Red Hills in Chennai
articles.description: Chennai: A 56-year-old man was charred to death after a cooking gas cylinder exploded at his house near Red Hills early Thursday.Police identified the.

articles.title: BC panel seeks clarity on process of Census ph 1
articles.description: Hyderabad: Telangana BC Commission chairman G Niranjan has written to Telangana Census director Bharati Hollikeri expressing concern over the absence .

articles.title: Our duty to confront political violence in America
articles.description: On Saturday, the unthinkable happened again. Another attempt was made on the life of President Trump, this time at the White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner, an event I was attending along with Cabinet members and many of my colleagues in the House and Senate. Let that sink in: An event meant to ...

articles.title: UK: ‘Islamic sect’ members arrested following allegations of sexual abuse and slavery
articles.description: Over 500 officers stormed an "Islamic sect" in Crewe, arresting 10 members, including their spiritual leader, for serious crimes like rape, sexual assault, and human trafficking. The raid targeted the Ahmadi Religion of Peace and Light headquarters. Police are investigating allegations of forced marriage and slavery reported by a former member.

articles.title: IPL-goers get free metro ride to and from Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians match on May 2
articles.description: Chennai: Metro train rides to and from M A Chidambaram Stadium will be free for those attending the 44th IPL match of the season, between Chennai Supe.

articles.title: BJP & BRS threaten stir over procurement delay
articles.description: Hyderabad: BRS and BJP leaders have alleged that delays in paddy procurement are pushing farmers into severe financial and mental distress, even as th.

articles.title: 'No CCTV turned off, all parties informed': EC dismiss TMC's tampering claims after Kolkata strongroom drama
articles.description: West Bengal’s chief electoral officer Manoj Kumar Agarwal said live footage from all eight strong rooms was being continuously telecasted.

articles.title: Amend pregnancy termination law of rape survivors, says SC
articles.description: The Supreme Court has asked the government to remove time limits for rape survivors to get abortions. This comes after a plea from AIIMS regarding a 15-year-old rape survivor's late-term pregnancy. The court stressed that the law must prioritize the dignity of survivors.

articles.title: Cyber Police Station building inaugurated in Latur
Shankaracharya inaugurates eye hospital in Saharsa
articles.description: Shankaracharya inaugurates eye hospital in Saharsa

articles.title: Opposition rejects exit polls: AIADMK confident of repeating 2011, 2016 victory
articles.description: Chennai: Leaders of opposition parties in Tamil Nadu have rejected the exit polls that gave an edge to DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance, calling t.

articles.title: Will Sabarimala Case Pave Way For Uniform Civil Code? Call For 9-Judge Bench
articles.description: The third article of the five-part series highlights how, in absence of a Uniform Civil Code, courts had to strike down regressive customs

articles.title: ‘No pay, no work’ protest in Dimapur
articles.description: In a bold move, employees of the Dimapur Municipal Council are set to embark on an indefinite 'no pay, no work' protest beginning May 5. The protest stems from prolonged frustrations over unpaid wages and outstanding dues that have remained unresolved, despite repeated appeals for action. This decisive stance comes courtesy of the Dimapur Municipal Council Employees' Welfare Union.

articles.title: Trump task force finds Biden administration persecuted traditional Christians
articles.description: A Trump administration task force report accuses the Biden administration of systematically targeting traditional Christians and cites hundreds of cases as evidence.

articles.title: Activists seek CM help with teacher crisis in govt schools
articles.description: In a pressing letter to Jharkhand's Chief Minister, renowned economist Jean Drèze and activist Elina Horo have sounded the alarm on a deepening crisis: a teacher shortage in government schools that disproportionately affects marginalized communities. They emphasize that many primary schools struggle with only one teacher per class, violating the Right to Education Act.

articles.title: RJD targets EC & BJP over ‘SIR model’, alleges mandate theft
articles.description: In a bold accusation, the RJD has called out the Election Commission for allegedly orchestrating rigging efforts in the West Bengal elections using a 'SIR model.' They claim that the manipulation of voter lists mirrors tactics previously seen in Bihar. The party argues that the BJP is desperately trying to usurp the public's will after facing widespread rejection.

articles.title: May 1 war powers deadline lands: Will Trump end the Iran war or challenge Congress authority?
articles.description: The conflict, which began on February 28 with US-Israel strikes on Iran, is now colliding with a decades-old law meant to limit presidential war powers.

articles.title: Students who were asked to remove janivara and hijab during CET to get another chance to write exam
articles.description: The Karnataka government is planning to allow students who were asked to remove their janivara (sacred thread) and hijab during the recently held Common Entrance Test (CET) to appear for the exam again.

articles.title: BJP Backs Jamia VC After ‘All Indians Share Mahadev DNA’ Remark Sparks Row
articles.description: Jamia Millia Islamia VC says all Indians share Mahadev DNA at RSS linked event, BJP backs remarks while SFI calls them unscientific and alleges crackdown on protesting students.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee dismisses exit projections, claims Trinamool will win over 226 seats
articles.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has rejected exit poll predictions. She stated these projections were released on BJP's orders to weaken her party. Banerjee also accused Union Home Minister Amit Shah and central forces of acting as BJP agents. She questioned Prime Minister Narendra Modi's remarks about poll results strengthening India's resolve while voting was still underway.

articles.title: Naval Anti-ship Missile : Navy, Drdo Demonstrate Salvo Launch That Can Sink Ships
articles.description: The Indian Navy and DRDO successfully conducted the first salvo firing of the Naval Anti‐Ship Missile–Short Range (NASM‐SR) from a Sea King helicopter off Odisha’s coast. Two missiles were launched in quick succession, proving the system’s salvo capability that overwhelms enemy defenses. Telemetry confirmed objectives were met, with strikes at the vulnerable waterline of target vessels. NASM‐SR is a fire‐and‐forget missile with post‐launch lock‐on ability, carrying a 100 kg warhead and reaching 55 km at high subsonic speeds while flying low to evade detection. Its long‐burn sustainer ensures extended powered flight. Critical systems were developed indigenously with industry support. Work is also underway on a longer‐range variant, NASM‐MR, showcased on the Navy’s MiG‐29K at Aero India 2025.

articles.title: 'We Will Win Over 226 Seats, Don't Trust Exit Polls': Mamata Banerjee On Bengal Election
articles.description: West Bengal Chief Minister and Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee

articles.title: Why Axis My India won't release Bengal exit poll results: ‘Respondent hesitancy, high refusal'
articles.description: The pollster said the high degree of hesitancy and refusal rate "exceeded historical norms and introduces a high degree of non-response bias".

articles.title: Palaniswami rejects exit polls; says AIADMK will win in 210 constituencies
articles.description: Palaniswami dismisses exit polls, asserting AIADMK will win 210 constituencies and form the government in Tamil Nadu.

articles.title: West Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 | Exit poll predictions aired at 'BJP instructions' to demoralise TMC workers: Mamata
articles.description: Assembly Polls Controversy: Mamata Banerjee accuses BJP of orchestrating exit poll narratives to demoralise TMC workers ahead of 2026 Elections.

articles.title: Canada opens up eligibility requirements to become a Canadian citizen
articles.description: Millions of Americans may qualify for dual Canadian citizenship under a recent change to Canada's requirements that has led to a surge in applications, multiple news outlets report.

articles.title: Kerala | PV Anvar vows to shave head if defeated in Beypore Assembly poll; challenges Riyas
articles.description: Kerala polls: UDF-backed PV Anvar challenges minister PA Mohamed Riyas with a head-shave dare if defeated in Beypore, escalating campaign rhetoric as voting nears, amid claims over margin, political shifts and contest in LDF stronghold.

articles.title: Supreme Court reserves for verdict Pawan Khera’s plea for pre-arrest bail in Assam case
articles.description: Supreme Court reserves decision on Pawan Khera's anticipatory bail plea amid allegations involving Assam CM's wife and multiple passports.

articles.title: Chic! Macron replies to King Charles' 'Americans speaking French' jibe
Persian Gulf not theater to impose foreign will: Iranian President
articles.description: Persian Gulf not theater to impose foreign will: Iranian President

articles.title: Former MLA Bachchu Kadu joins Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena for Maharashtra MLC polls
articles.description: Former MLA Bachchu Kadu has officially joined Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena, securing a nomination for the MLC polls. Kadu stated Shinde accepted his conditions, including farmer welfare and strengthening the Divyang ministry. His Prahar Janshakti Party will continue its social work, while political members will now support Sena.

articles.title: Gurgaon deputy mayor polls deferred again as mayor skips special house meeting
articles.description: Elections for senior deputy mayor and deputy mayor in the MCG were postponed on Thursday as Mayor Raj Rani Malhotra was absent due to medical reasons. This marks the second failed attempt to elect these key positions, with the polls now rescheduled within two weeks following a High Court directive.

articles.title: War with Iran may have cost the US $40-$50 billion so far: Report
articles.description: While a top Pentagon official has said the war in West Asia has cost the US $25 billion so far, the estimate does not account for damage to American bases in the region

articles.title: Ex-Kerala MLA P.V. Anvar vows to ‘shave head’ if defeated in Assembly polls
articles.description: Ex-MLA P.V. Anvar vows to shave his head if he loses the Beypore Assembly polls, challenging rival Mohamed Riyas.

articles.title: From Honey Singh to Badshah and Nora Fatehi: Are ‘objectionable’ songs influencing youth behaviour?
articles.description: Authorities across India are taking action against songs and content deemed vulgar and glorifying crime, leading to takedowns and FIRs. This crackdown, involving police, courts, and regulatory bodies, sparks debate on balancing creative freedom with social responsibility, particularly concerning youth influence.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections 2026 | CM M K Stalin meets senior DMK leaders to review victory prospects ahead of May 4 results
articles.description: Tamil Nadu elections: CM MK Stalin meets senior DMK leaders to assess victory prospects after exit polls hint at return to power, with focus on counting day strategy, booth agents and safeguards as results are set to be declared on May 4

articles.title: Iran warns of new weapon that could give US a ‘heart attack’
articles.description: Iran’s Navy Commander Shahram Irani said they will soon deploy a new weapon “right next to” enemies, adding he hopes it won’t cause a “heart attack.”

articles.title: TVK confident of forming government with 150 seats: Anand Mohan
articles.description: TVK leader K.K. Anand Mohan dismisses exit polls for the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, asserting the party, led by Thalapathy Vijay, will form the government with a clear majority of over 150 seats on May 4.

articles.title: Jairam Ramesh slams Centre, awaits details on 2027 caste census
articles.description: Congress MP Jairam Ramesh slammed the Modi government, stating details of the caste census announced a year ago are still awaited. He demanded an apology from PM Modi for the 'urban naxal' jibe and criticised the lack of dialogue with opposition.

articles.title: Hard work of people is nation's greatest strength, says PM Modi
articles.description: PM Narendra Modi lauded the hard work and energy of Indian citizens as the nation's greatest strength, linking their efforts to India's development. His remarks came after polling concluded in 5 states, with Modi thanking West Bengal voters.

articles.title: BJP will win Bengal with two-thirds majority: Ram Kripal Yadav
articles.description: Following exit polls, BJP leader Ram Kripal Yadav stated the party will secure a two-thirds majority in West Bengal. He also expressed confidence in forming a government again in Assam, asserting that a change in Bengal is certain.

articles.title: 76th FIFA Congress row: Iran officials denied entry to Canada over IRGC terrorist label
articles.description: FIFA delegates meet in Vancouver amid 2026 World Cup tensions. Iran officials barred from Canada, Russia's return debated, and World Cup prize controversy.

articles.title: US-Iran war cost: $25bn or just a low-ball figure? What the Pentagon told Congress
articles.description: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion, though sources suggest the true figure could reach $50 billion due to unincluded repair costs. Officials defended the ongoing conflict, calling it an "existential fight" and dismissing concerns of it becoming a "quagmire."

articles.title: High Court reverses acquittal of trio, convicts them over organising pro-Palestinian procession to Istana
articles.description: Justice See Kee Oon found that the judge in the women's trial had erred in his application of the law in acquitting them.

articles.title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani Urges King Charles To Return Koh-I-Noor Diamond To India
articles.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has reignited the global debate over the Koh-i-Noor diamond after saying he would encourage King Charles III to return the historic jewel to India during the monarch’s New York visit. The 105-carat diamond, presented to Queen Victoria in 1850 and now displayed at the Tower of London, remains one of the most disputed colonial-era artifacts in the British royal collection. India has repeatedly demanded its return, arguing that the Koh-i-Noor symbolizes colonial exploitation and cultural loss. Mamdani’s comments add a new political dimension to the long-running controversy, connecting modern diplomacy, monarchy, and post-colonial justice. In this video, we analyze why the Koh-i-Noor still matters, what King Charles could face diplomatically, and whether Britain may ever return the diamond.

articles.title: Bhojshala complex registered as 'mosque' in old revenue records: Muslim side to MP high court
articles.description: Madhya Pradesh High Court heard arguments on the Bhojshala Temple-Kamal Maula Mosque dispute. The Muslim side argued the site is historically a mosque, with no clear evidence of a Saraswati temple by Raja Bhoj. They cited revenue records and continuous occupation, asserting legal rights for mosque management. The court continues hearing the matter.
